finding: broker batch execute uses --run-command as a shell string for generated build/projection writes, which is fragile on Windows quoting and forces AI operators to debug shell syntax instead of declaring an executable command contract.
expectedBehavior: Generated-write execution should accept an argv-array or JSON command manifest surface, preserve exact arguments cross-platform, and expose first-layer skill/CLI guidance so operators do not rediscover shell quoting rules.
evidenceOrRepro: ATM-GOV-0187 implementation and test work required changing the test command to quote process.execPath for Windows; later runner build measurement also required switching between npm, npm.cmd, and shell:true surfaces before a valid run.
followUp: Add a follow-up task to introduce --run-command-json or command manifest support, update help/skills, and keep --run-command as a compatibility shortcut with explicit Windows warnings.
